25.reason: [Error: The problem requires that values greater than or equal to the root go to the right child, but this condition only handles strictly greater values, so equal values are not inserted correctly.]

reasons.buggy-deepseek-chat-1: 1.0 , reason:  [Error: The problem requires that values greater than or equal to the root go to the right child, but this condition only handles strictly greater values, so equal values are not inserted correctly.]
reasons.buggy-deepseek-chat-2: 1.0 , reason:  [Error: The problem requires that values greater than or equal to the root go to the right child, but this condition only handles strictly greater values, missing the case when item == p->data, which should also go to the right subtree.]
reasons.buggy-deepseek-reasoner-1: 1.0 , reason:  Error: Should handle item >= p->data (duplicates go to right). Use else instead of else if to include equality.
reasons.buggy-deepseek-reasoner-2: 1.0 , reason:  [Error: should be 'else' (or 'item>=p->data') because equal values must go to the right child per problem specification]
reasons.buggy-qwen3.6-plus-1: 1.0 , reason:  The problem specifies that the right child's value should be greater than or equal to the root's value. Using '>' ignores duplicate values. It should be 'else' or 'else if(item>=p->data)' to correctly insert duplicates into the right subtree.
reasons.buggy-qwen3.6-plus-2: 1.0 , reason:  The problem specifies that the right child's value should be greater than or equal to the root. This condition ignores duplicate values (item == p->data), causing them to be lost. It should be changed to 'else' or 'item >= p->data'.
